sessions.title: Edge Inference at Scale: 1-Bit LLMs in the Field
sessions.description: How Red Hat and Intel are bringing AI inference to edge deployments using 1-bit ternary models that run on CPU-only hardware with minimal resources.

sessions.title: Building RAG Systems for Disconnected Environments
sessions.description: Hands-on workshop on building retrieval-augmented generation systems that work entirely offline using lightweight embedding models and local vector stores.

sessions.title: BitNet: From Research Paper to Production Deployment
sessions.description: Deep dive into BitNet 1.58-bit quantization, ternary weight inference, and deploying Microsoft's BitNet b1.58-2B-4T model on Intel Xeon hardware.

sessions.title: OpenShift at the Edge: Patterns and Anti-Patterns
sessions.description: Lessons learned deploying Red Hat OpenShift to edge sites: single-node deployments, disconnected registries, fleet management with RHACM.

sessions.title: SMS as an AI Interface: Reaching the Last Mile
sessions.description: Why SMS remains the most accessible communication channel and how to build AI-powered SMS services for communities with limited connectivity.

sessions.title: Intel AMX and AVX-512 for AI Workloads
sessions.description: How Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512 instructions accelerate integer-only inference for quantized models on Xeon processors.

sessions.title: Disaster Relief Technology: AI for Humanitarian Response
sessions.description: Case studies of deploying technology in disaster zones — connectivity challenges, power constraints, and how edge AI can provide critical information when infrastructure fails.

sessions.title: Container Security on UBI9: From Dev to Edge
sessions.description: Building secure container images with Red Hat Universal Base Image 9, vulnerability scanning, and supply chain security for edge deployments.

sessions.title: Scaling Edge Nodes: Fleet Management at 1000 Sites
sessions.description: How to manage thousands of edge nodes with GitOps, RHACM, and zero-touch provisioning — keeping models updated and services healthy across a global fleet.

sessions.title: Panel: AI at the Edge — From Lab to Field
sessions.description: Industry leaders discuss the real-world challenges and opportunities of deploying AI inference at the edge, from hardware constraints to community impact.

sessions.title: Hands-On: Build Your Own SMS AI Assistant
sessions.description: 3-hour lab where you build and deploy your own SMS-based AI assistant using BitNet, RAG, and FastAPI. Bring your laptop — you'll leave with a working system.

sessions.title: Hands-On: Intel Xeon Optimization for AI Inference
sessions.description: Optimize AI model inference on Intel Xeon processors. Profile performance, enable AMX instructions, and benchmark quantized models.

sessions.title: The Future of Open Source AI
sessions.description: Where open source AI is heading — model licensing, community-driven development, and the democratization of AI through projects like llama.cpp, BitNet, and Hugging Face.

sessions.title: Closing Keynote: Technology for Everyone, Everywhere
sessions.description: A vision for technology that serves everyone — bridging the digital divide with edge computing, open source, and community-driven innovation.
